transcript: Speaker A: Speak fast and clear: I left my village and came to a big city. Leaving my family behind. Leaving my Father and Mother behind. People said I won't succeed. They said a village boy doesn't belong here. They said English is not for someone like me. But I refused to believe them. Because I carry something they can't see. My father's sacrifice. My mother's prayers. Their tired hands and sleepless nights, All of it, for me. I didn't come this far to go back empty-handed. Some days, the city feels too loud. The language feels too foreign. I open my books and my mind goes blank. I miss home. I miss them. But then I remember why I left. I open MySivi App. Just 10 minutes. That's all I need. The AI teacher doesn't judge me. Doesn't make me feel small. Every mistake? A lesson. Every session? One step closer to them. I'm not competing with anyone in this city. I'm just better than I was yesterday. When doubt creeps in, I open the app. When loneliness hits at midnight, I press start. Because I made a promise. Not just to myself. To them. Today I practiced on MySivi. Tomorrow I'll practice again. And one day, maybe soon, I'll call my father and speak with confidence. I'll walk into that interview room with my head held high. I'll send money home. I'll make them proud. Not because I was the smartest. Not because it was easy. But because I didn't give up. MySivi didn't just teach me English. It reminded me who I'm doing this for. So today, I choose to keep going. Because Mummy, Papa, Your son is still trying. And your son is not done yet.
